breakClass: Nested collection / sub-entity projection EF Core 10 cannot translate: an inline nested `Field = (from x in db.Y join ... select new {...}).ToList()` (or a `let img = db.Y.Where(...).Select(...).ToList()` that is then projected) placed inside a SERVER-SIDE outer `select new {...}` that is enumerated by `.ToList()`/`.FirstOrDefault()` with no prior `.AsEnumerable()`/`.ToList()` boundary. EF6 ran these (separate per-row queries / query-splitting); EF Core 10 throws `The LINQ expression ... could not be translated` / `ProjectionBindingExpression could not be translated` -> HTTP 500. Two confirmed systemic sub-classes: (A) Common.cs PDF/print builders projecting img + bundle (+inner bimg) collections; (B) process-flow/status/service-report master grids projecting AssignType/AssignedUsers/Depts/Desgn/contacts/itemsused collections. NOTE: the HireReturn/CrossHireReturn/PackingList pdfItem builders and the MyReports/Leads/ProTask *dashboards* are SAFE because the outer source is already materialized (`from o in v` after `.ToList()`, or nested `.ToList()` sits after `.Distinct().ToList().Select(...)`), making the nested query a client-side N+1, not a 500.
why: v stays IQueryable through .Where/.OrderBy and is enumerated server-side by v.Skip().Take().ToList() at line 96 (no AsEnumerable boundary). The projection emits two nested collections per row (Depts, Desgn) via inline (from z ... select new{...}).ToList(). EF Core 10 cannot translate these inline collection projections -> 500 on the AMC Status master DataTable grid. Legacy AMCStatusController has the byte-identical Depts/Desgn projection (port regression).
reasoning:
I independently read the full method GetAmcStatus (QuickNetCore/Controllers/AMCStatusController.cs lines 44-98) and confirmed both triage criteria.

(a) SERVER-SIDE: `var v` (line 59-80) is built directly from `db.AmcStatuss` join `db.Users` â€” all of db.AmcStatuss, db.AmcStatusDepts, db.Departments, db.AmcStatusDesgs, db.Designations are real EF DbSets (verified in Models/ApplicationDbContext.cs lines 43-92), so `v` is an IQueryable<anonymous>. It is never materialized: line 86 applies `v.Where(...)` (still IQueryable), line 92 applies `v.AsQueryable().OrderBy(...)` (still IQueryable), line 95 `v.Count()` runs server-side, and line 96 `var data = v.Skip(skip).Take(pageSize).ToList()` is the FIRST and only enumeration boundary. There is NO prior `.ToList()`/`.AsEnumerable()`/`.AsAsyncEnumerable()`. The two nested fragments `Depts = (from z ... select new {DeptName=...}).ToList()` and `Desgn = (from z ... select new {DesgnName=...}).ToList()` are inline nested collection projections sitting inside the server-side outer `select new {...}`. They are NOT group-aggregates over an IGrouping, NOT standalone top-level locals, NOT post-materialization. The hunter's claim is accurate.

(b) WOULD 500 in EF Core 10: this is exactly the confirmed systemic sub-class (B) â€” a master-grid projecting collection sub-entities (Depts/Desgn) per row. EF Core 10 cannot translate a nested `(from ... select new {...}).ToList()` embedded inside a server-side outer projection that is enumerated by `.ToList()`; it throws "The LINQ expression ... could not be translated" / ProjectionBindingExpression at query-compilation time, before any rows are read. So it 500s on any non-trivial load (and even structurally regardless of data, since translation precedes execution) â†’ HIGH severity. The AMC Status index DataTable POSTs to this endpoint on every page view.

PORT REGRESSION confirmed: the legacy EF6 source at _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/AMCStatusController.cs lines 49-87 contains the byte-identical Depts/Desgn projection consumed identically via `v.Skip().Take().ToList()`. EF6 executed it as separate per-row (N+1) subqueries; the EF Core 10 port inherited the shape verbatim but the provider no longer supports it. This is a true port regression, not a new bug.

Verdict: CONFIRMED. I could not refute it â€” the fragment is positively server-side and positively non-translatable in EF Core 10.
